sglang.bodyExcerpt: From the nsys profiling of DeepSeek H200 R1, it can be observed that the Triton version of the _per_token_group_quant_fp8 kernel is a relatively time-consuming operation, accounting for 2.1%. Upon examining its number of blocks and threads, it is found that the number of blocks is large while the nu

sglang.title: Tune MI300X fused MoE Triton kernel JSON config.
sglang.author: whchung
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Modifications
Align the JSON configs between MI300X and Radeon Graphics for BS=64, E=256, N=256, dtype=fp8_w8a8, block_shape=[128,128] case. The best tuned config was submitted in #3418 but it was only for Radeon Graphics. Let MI300X adopt the same configuration in this PR.

sglang.title: Make NCCL NVLS configurable
sglang.author: MrAta
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When available, NCCL NVLS can be used for in switch allreduce. This is especially important for long context and prefill heavy requests.
In our benchmarks on a node with 8 H100 GPUs with nvlink connectivity, we observe about 20% speed up in all reduce for both attention and MLP allreduce

vllm.author: comaniac

vllm.title: [executor] init `local_rank` as device index
vllm.author: MengqingCao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
What does this pr do
This pr initailizing local_rank by the rank specified in argument device if it exists.
FIX #12967
Bug description
While specifying device to cards except card-0, as following code, there'll cause an device conflict because the tensors (such as attn_bias) will be put on card-0 by

vllm.title: [ROCm] Using a more precise memory profiling
vllm.author: gshtras
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
To fix another ROCm regression from #11809
Currently the profiling does not take into account padded memory (torch.nn.functional.pad), so if it is used (currently used in ROCm/vllm for fp8 and MoE), the amount of available cache memory would be computed incorrectly and OOM would happen.

vllm.title: [Build] Fix cuda link target of cumem_allocator in CPU env
vllm.author: guoyuhong
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #12862
It looks like that the build system could not find -lcuda correctly in the CPU container.
list(APPEND CUMEM_LIBS cuda) is too simple for a CPU build env, ld cannot find the location. It is better to use CUDA::cuda_driver which is a full path.

vllm.title: [Platform] add pre_register_and_update function
vllm.author: wangxiyuan
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
When a quantization method is implemented out-of-tree in platform plugin, there comes a problem that no place to load the register method in vLLM.
For example:
# In plugin repo:
@register_quantization_config("new_method")
def NewQuantConfig:
    ...

# in vLLM:
from vllm import LLM
LLM(model="./opt-

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Do not use resource module on Windows (#12858)
vllm.author: MoonRide303
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Python resource module is supported only on *nix OSes - it should never be imported on Windows.
FIX #12858

vllm.title: [BugFix] Pop instead of del CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES
vllm.author: HollowMan6
vllm.bodyExcerpt: In case RAY_EXPERIMENTAL_NOSET_CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES is set, then ray won't manipulate CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES. If it happens that the node env also doesn't set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES, a KeyError will be thrown as we directly access it with os.environ["CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES"]

vllm.title: Fix initializing GGUF weights for ColumnParallelLinear when using tensor parallel > 1
vllm.author: SzymonOzog
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Currently there would be size mismatches when loading the weight because we don't take TP into consideration when materializing gguf weights. This fixes it by reducing the output dimension by TP size

vllm.title: [CI/Build][Bugfix] Fix CPU backend default threads num
vllm.author: bigPYJ1151
vllm.bodyExcerpt: MultiprocessingDistributedExecutor will set the default openmp threads num to 1 and leads to slow CPU CI testing. This PR bypasses it by setting default OMP_NUM_THREADS ahead.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Guided decoding falls back to outlines when fails to import xgrammar
vllm.author: terrytangyuan
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This fixes the issue when xgrammar module cannot be imported successfully for some reason, e.g. triton is not available. This fallback allows users to still use guided decoding when xgrammar cannot be used.

the pre-commit checks. Its better to have that at the end instead of the
middle.
I also included some comments to remind future editors of the file to keep this
e

vllm.title: [RFC][vllm-API] Support tokenizer registry for customized tokenizer in vLLM
vllm.author: youngkent

To allow customized on-demand registration of tokenizer, this diff is to propose a tokenizer registry in vllm, similar to model registry.

Created a TokenizerBase as the standard vLLM tokenizer API
Mistral tokenizer now inherit from TokenizerBase
Support TokenizerRegistry for register and get tokeni

vllm.title: [Model] IBM/NASA Prithvi Geospatial model
vllm.author: christian-pinto
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This pull request proposes a first integration of Prithvi, a non-language model to vLLM. This is related to the discussion we had in a previous RFC #11065.
Prithvi is a non-autoregressive model ingesting torch tensors in input and generating on (or a list of) torch tensors. As suggested in the discu

This PR is a follow-up of #11277 . It improves code quality and efficiency, and enables kernel to process larger inputs.
Following things are done in this PR:

fix 2 tiling issues triggered when seqlen_q (i.e. chunk size in chunked-prefill) is larger than 128 and 512
get rid of the limit of
